**NH REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, AUGUST 3rd**
|
---
**_A Note From Wayne_**
Dear Derek,

I want to begin this week's message by thanking those who have been involved with our finance efforts. Appreciation goes out to Finance Chairman Jim Morgan and everyone on the finance committee, and former Chairman Jeanie Forrester and everyone on the event committee for the "roast" honoring Governor John H. Sununu. Their efforts are invaluable to the financial well-being of the Party.

The Belknap County Republican Committee generously gave the Republican State Committee a donation of $2,500.00, which is over ten percent of their treasury and has challenged every other county and city committee to donate at least ten percent of their treasury. I understand that this will not be possible in all cases, but I do appreciate what Belknap and the counties of Hillsborough, Cheshire, Rockingham and the Derry Committees have done. This support and the ongoing hard work of all of the committees are invaluable as we move ahead in this election cycle. Thank you all!

The New Hampshire Democrats had a whirlwind of negative press this past week. In their gubernatorial primary, Democrat candidates Molly Kelly and Steve Marchand continue to move to the left, trying to please a Democrat base that wants socialism and more government control of our lives. In their hotly contested Congressional District 01 race, the Democrat establishment continues to revive the faltering campaign of Executive Councilor Chris Pappas, worried that out-of-stater Maura Sullivan or one of the liberal progressives running to his left will beat him in the September Primary. Senator Shaheen is scheduled to endorse his campaign next week, the latest sign that elite Democrats are worried about Pappas' chances.

Finally, last night, Senate Democrat leader Jeff Woodburn was arrested for 9 counts of charges for domestic abuse and violence allegations. [My statement on the matter can be found here.](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=3qP3xl6Iy86fMdiFlFopyNBdZmBUvkQRAz1p77RRt3KRWUFl0pTmtf3XMyPuoIVQZaniLv0GZ6mugscwS3671w-3D-3D_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30MnhDiGZTkBpAoT2IA-2B52fQWEYPJw2dZEbkgfpuo5Ys-2BSonNKeCcAt91XKJgD9W2V-2F04f0rKDnFQ2E076hErIBIXjWa0nWp1xbD1T6A6fKpN2MfkCQu2tir-2FW38LBC1OieGxV-2Fff-2FPcghnrGUXk1SBwwlYn-2Bjuo-2BSi4nhZ5Ok7gwHv-2BhbxcgsvksXRKNhFsz2hXIlgkIuXkm-2BEpomkbtikV98zPUgOncj6CakLCdGM3skWMyZgaZWnbV5RC9z0VYDSxFMnG8eZG0INFu47w8-2BNxQ-3D-3D)

clean_body: | | nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, AUGUST 3rd | A Note From Wayne Dear Derek, I want to begin this week's message by thanking those who have been involved with our finance efforts. Appreciation goes out to Finance Chairman Jim Morgan and everyone on the finance committee, and former Chairman Jeanie Forrester and everyone on the event committee for the "roast" honoring Governor John H. Sununu. Their efforts are invaluable to the financial well-being of the Party. The Belknap County Republican Committee generously gave the Republican State Committee a donation of $2,500.00, which is over ten percent of their treasury and has challenged every other county and city committee to donate at least ten percent of their treasury. I understand that this will not be possible in all cases, but I do appreciate what Belknap and the counties of Hillsborough, Cheshire, Rockingham and the Derry Committees have done. This support and the ongoing hard work of all of the committees are invaluable as we move ahead in this election cycle. Thank you all! The New Hampshire Democrats had a whirlwind of negative press this past week. In their gubernatorial primary, Democrat candidates Molly Kelly and Steve Marchand continue to move to the left, trying to please a Democrat base that wants socialism and more government control of our lives. In their hotly contested Congressional District 01 race, the Democrat establishment continues to revive the faltering campaign of Executive Councilor Chris Pappas, worried that out-of-stater Maura Sullivan or one of the liberal progressives running to his left will beat him in the September Primary. Senator Shaheen is scheduled to endorse his campaign next week, the latest sign that elite Democrats are worried about Pappas' chances. Finally, last night, Senate Democrat leader Jeff Woodburn was arrested for 9 counts of charges for domestic abuse and violence allegations. While the New Hampshire Democrats have these political struggles, New Hampshire Republicans are laying the groundwork for victory across the Granite State in many ways. name: Doug Jones
subject: What happened in Alabama
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Kaine for Virginia
body:
| | Derek, hope is an interesting thing.

As a US attorney, I prosecuted two of the KKK members responsible for the 1963 bombing of a church in Birmingham, Alabama, that killed four little black girls. **The men who planted that bomb wanted to crush the _hope_ rising within the African American community as a result of the Civil Rights Movement. **

This pattern of violence following surges of hope continues today: We see it in attacks on mosques and synagogues, against immigrants, against members of the LGBTQ community.

**We saw it when white supremacists and neo-Nazis descended on Charlottesville,[one year ago this month. ](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f12f6/3e0906/6fb3b7a1/3e3500b1/1147965575/VEsH/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/sign-the-reject-hate-pledge?source=20180804_1_PET_DJ_Jones-Surrogate-NS&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=1+-+one+year+ago+this+month+&utm_campaign=20180804_1_PET_DJ_Jones-Surrogate-NS")**

The only way to stop this pattern is to stand up and show that violence will never stop us from fighting for equality. I'm proud to stand against hate -- and I know my friend Tim is, too. **Will you add your name and pledge to stand alongside us?** | [**SIGN THE PLEDGE: REJECT HATE**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f12f6/3e0906/6fb3b7a1/3e3500b0/1147965575/VEsE/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/sign-the-reject-hate-pledge?source=20180804_1_PET_DJ_Jones-Surrogate-NS&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=2+-+SIGN+THE+PLEDGE+REJECT+HATE&utm_campaign=20180804_1_PET_DJ_Jones-Surrogate-NS")
---
One thing Tim says a lot is that "_equality is America 's North Star_" \-- and he's devoted his career to following that star in Virginia, just like I have in Alabama.

My state -- and your state -- have come a long way since the dark days of that church bombing in 1963. But we still have a ways to go, and we can't let anybody or anything turn us around.

**You saw what happened in Alabama -- we rejected hate, and those who embraced it, by heading to the ballot box on[December 12th of last year. And we can do that again in Virginia on November 6th of this year:](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f12f6/3e0906/6fb3b7a1/3e3500b7/1147965575/VEsF/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/sign-the-reject-hate-pledge?source=20180804_1_PET_DJ_Jones-Surrogate-NS&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=3+-+December+12th+of+last+year+And+we+can+do&utm_campaign=20180804_1_PET_DJ_Jones-Surrogate-NS")**
[**SIGN THE PLEDGE: REJECT HATE**](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f12f6/3e0906/6fb3b7a1/3e3500b6/1147965575/VEsC/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/sign-the-reject-hate-pledge?source=20180804_1_PET_DJ_Jones-Surrogate-NS&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=4+-+SIGN+THE+PLEDGE+REJECT+HATE&utm_campaign=20180804_1_PET_DJ_Jones-Surrogate-NS")
---
Hope represents the best of this nation's ideals. Like I have said before: Hope is durable, but it's not invincible. And if we fail to protect it, we all suffer the consequences.

[Tim and I are protecting hope and standing against hate. I _hope_ you'll stand with us. ](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f12f6/3e0906/6fb3b7a1/3e3500b5/1147965575/VEsD/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/sign-the-reject-hate-pledge?source=20180804_1_PET_DJ_Jones-Surrogate-NS&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=5+-+Tim+and+I+are+protecting+hope+and+standi&utm_campaign=20180804_1_PET_DJ_Jones-Surrogate-NS")

_Thanks for all you 're doing to help keep my friend in the Senate, _

_Doug Jones_

[https://secure.timkaine.com/Pledge-to-Reject-Hate](https://secure.timkaine.com/page/m/330f12f6/3e0906/6fb3b7a1/3e3500b4/1147965575/VEsA/ "https://secure.timkaine.com/page/s/sign-the-reject-hate-pledge?source=20180804_1_PET_DJ_Jones-Surrogate-NS&utm_medium=email&utm_source=kaine&utm_content=6+-+httpssecuretimkainecomPledgetoRejectHate&utm_campaign=20180804_1_PET_DJ_Jones-Surrogate-NS")

disclaimer_text: Paid for by Tina Smith for Minnesota
body:
This is shameful and we cannot let Betsy DeVos and the Trump Administration get away with it.

| [](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4a405/c1f7015/6afdb519/11426bed/3096588401/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjU1NzgzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiIifQ==/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_1807_devos-student-loans?source=rtw_20180804_em&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=55783&firstname=") _Washington Post 6/25_ : "Education Secretary Betsy DeVos moved Wednesday to make it harder for students who say they were defrauded by colleges to erase their debts." **Join Tina and tell Betsy DeVos to protect students, not for-profit colleges:** [Add Your Name](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4a405/c1f7015/6afdb519/11426bed/3096588401/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjU1NzgzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiIifQ==/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_1807_devos-student-loans?source=rtw_20180804_em&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=55783&firstname=") Friend, Betsy DeVos is systematically rewriting Obama Administration protections that were put in place to protect students from predatory for-profit colleges. These students -- many of whom are veterans and older Americans -- came to these institutions looking to make their lives better. Instead, colleges defrauded students, leaving them with a worthless degree and tens of thousands in debt they may never be able to pay off. **This is shameful and we cannot let Betsy DeVos and the Trump Administration get away with it.[Add your name to my petition and tell DeVos to reverse her proposed rule and stand up for students, not for-profit colleges:](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4a405/c1f7015/6afdb519/11426bed/3096588401/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjU1NzgzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiIifQ==/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_1807_devos-student-loans?source=rtw_20180804_em&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=55783&firstname=")** [Add Your Name](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4a405/c1f7015/6afdb519/11426bed/3096588401/VEsC/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjU1NzgzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiIifQ==/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_1807_devos-student-loans?source=rtw_20180804_em&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=55783&firstname=") This package of rules that was "applauded" by the for-profit college industry will be devastating if it goes into effect. These rules will:
* Give borrowers less time to apply for relief.
* Allow for-profit colleges to force students into an arbitration process that favors the colleges' interests.
* No longer offer automatic loan forgiveness in the event the school shuts down -- leaving students holding loans with no degree.

We have limited time to speak up before these rules will go into effect. That's why we're asking you to act right now. **[Will you sign our petition to Betsy DeVos and tell her to back down from these rules immediately? Add your name here.](https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/m/4bc4a405/c1f7015/6afdb519/11426bed/3096588401/VEsD/p/eyJKU1ZGVFVGSlRDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVmFTVkFsSlE9PSI6IjU1NzgzIiwiSlNWR1NWSlRWRTVCVFVVbEpRPT0iOiIifQ==/ "https://action.tinaforminnesota.com/page/s/rtw_1807_devos-student-loans?source=rtw_20180804_em&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=55783&firstname=")** Thank you, Tina

4x Match Extended: Chip in Now

Fellow Conservative,

Thanks to support from thousands of conservatives across America, we hit our fundraising goal for July. Your support means a lot to us. We couldn’t have done it without you!

We still have a lot of work left to do in order to catch up to the Democrats’ fundraising lead. Can we count on you to help us start August strong? Chip in now!

If we do not step up now, we could lose to Nancy Pelosi, Chuck Schumer, and their progressive allies. This would be a disaster for our Making America Great Again agenda.

body:
There's only one Democrat in this race.

| [](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a9b8/7ab0e2c4/7fcecac2/349b3214/2281949487/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/benj-one-democrat?refcode=OHQ08048A") Friend, Today, Larry Hogan will be celebrating with some "Democrats for Hogan" at his Baltimore office opening. But the truth is, these "Democrats" don't reflect the diversity of our state, many of them have a history of endorsing Republicans, and many others have been appointed by Hogan for other positions. He thinks that this event will show he understands the people of Baltimore and of this state, but it shows just the opposite. 1. The GOP's anti-middle-class tax scheme gave the majority of benefits to corporations and the 1%, not the people who need it most.

2. Because of the deficit that tax scheme created, the GOP has proposed devastating cuts to Medicare, Medicaid and Social Security.

3. Multiple attempts to repeal the Affordable Care Act have put the health care of millions of Americans at risk and forced premiums to spike.

The President's agenda makes it clear that he is out of touch with what Pennsylvanians truly need. My opponent, Congressman Barletta, is no better: he says he's running to "work for" President Trump, rather than working for the constituents he represents.

**The Trump administration 's agenda has already hurt middle-class families and workers across the Commonwealth, and we need to put a stop to it. body:
I'm rallying voters for Cori Bush and Abdul El-Sayed this weekend before their critical primary elections on Tuesday.
#
Flint, Michigan. Ferguson, Missouri. Friends, these communities are more than what you see on the news. **They are our hometowns - communities full of caring, compassionate people who are working for change.**
People across our country are fed up with the status quo, and these two communities are part of a broader movement fighting for political revolution and a government of, by, and for the people. This is where we are taking Our Revolution to continue the fight that Senator Bernie Sanders began.
**[I'm in St. Louis and Detroit this weekend, rallying voters for Cori Bush and Abdul El-Sayed before their critical primary elections on Tuesday. Can you pitch in $5 so that Our Revolution can continue to organize in your communities and fight for economic, racial, social, and environmental justice?](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a9f7/bed43eb/7e94d788/75fd752d/1537976413/VEsH/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/august-candidates?refcode=em180804-full")**
These candidates aren't taking a dime of corporate money. They are relying on you and me to make the difference in their grassroots campaigns. That is why Senator Sanders and Alexandria Ocasio-Cortez support them, why Linda Sarsour and Our Revolution board member Shailene Woodley will be in Michigan, and why thousands of people are turning out to rally alongside us.
Cori Bush and Abdul El-Sayed are fighting for Medicare for All, a $15 minimum wage, and criminal justice reform that addresses the injustices within the system. Their campaigns are led by committed volunteers and activists who are on the ground getting out the vote, and here's the kicker:
**There are more than 100 Our Revolution candidates and nearly 600 local groups fighting for progressive policies across our country at every level of government.**
**[The only way we can help them win is through all of us pitching in together to create a movement of millions of people for change. Can you help Our Revolution do that? Make a $5 donation today.](https://go.ourrevolution.com/page/m/2ba7a9f7/bed43eb/7e94d788/75fd752d/1537976413/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/august-candidates?refcode=em180804-full")**
Thank you for sustaining this movement for change. I'll be checking in again soon from the road.
In solidarity,
Nina Turner

[](https://secure.bobcasey.com/page/m/661e604f/51dbcc2a/18b8ddba/3a6c5f1/1533276503/VEsH/ "https://bobcasey.com/") President Trump was only in Pennsylvania for three hours on Thursday.

But, according to estimates from local GOP officials, that's all it took to raise _$1 million_ for Congressman Barletta's campaign against Bob Casey.

**_[That's why I'm asking you for money today -- it's going to take every supporter like you, giving $5, $25 or whatever you can, to stay on track.](https://secure.bobcasey. "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/bob-casey?refcode=em_20180804_fr2_v1s2&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500")_**

Team, the president's rally was basically a free hour of advertising for Barletta, broadcast on cable news and local stations across the state. Looking at Bob's ad budget, we need to raise an extra $12,000 today to begin to effectively push back.

We can't afford to stay silent on this: President Trump _lied_ about Bob and his record of helping working Pennsylvanians across the state. You and I know Bob is a champion for middle-class families, and now it's on us to make sure that every Pennsylvania voter knows that. We need to tell them about Bob's record of protecting miners' health care and making sure students are safe at school. They need to know that, just this week, the president signed Bob's bill to fund critical workforce training programs -- the first reauthorization of those programs in _twelve years_.

**Bob has a winning message, and after Trump's speech last night, it's never been more important to make sure that every voter hears it.
